revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Chihara Ōbaka Kofun]][[ja:茅原大墓古墳]]
{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan|qq=}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name =Chihara Ōbaka Kofun | native_name =茅原大墓古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Chihara Ohaka Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Chihara Ōbaka Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Chihara Ōbaka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.53673055555555|N|135.84722222222223|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Sakurai|qid=Q127026|1=Sakurai, Nara}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|qid=Q11480883|1=Scallop-shaped kofun}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]|qq=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|12.23|N|135|50|50|E|qq=}}|qq=}}|qq=}} {{nihongo|'''Chihara Ōbaka Kofun'''|茅原大墓古墳||qq=}} is an early [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Chihara neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|Sakurai, Nara|qid=Q127026|lt=Sakurai, Nara|1=Sakurai, Nara}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1982.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2039|title=茅原大墓古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024|qq=}}</ref> ==Overview== Chihara Ōbaka Kofun is located in the southeastern part of the Nara Basin, at the western foot of {{ill|Mount Miwa|en|Mount Miwa|qid=Q860627|lt=Mount Miwa|1=Mount Miwa}}. The area contains the Yamato Tomb Group, including the {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|en|Hashihaka Kofun|qid=Q11603420|lt=Hashihaka Kofun|1=Hashihaka Kofun}}, dating from early Kofun period, (3rd to the latter half of the 4th century); however, this ''kofun'' is from the middleof the Kofun period, no other major tombs from around the time of the construction of this tumulus are in the immediate vicinity. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1978. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|scallop-shaped kofun|帆立型古墳|||qq=}} orientated to the north. It as a length of 67 meters, with a posterior circular portion 56 meters in diameter and eight meters tall. The anterior portion has a maximum width of 29 meters, and is two meters tall. There is a reservoir pond on the west side of the tumulus, which is thought to be the surviving remains of a moat. The posterior mound is constructed in five tiers, with ''[[fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical and house-shaped {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} on the surface. In 2011, a ''haniwa'' from the late 4th century depicting a human head on a shield was found in the moat, and is the oldest anthropomorphic ''haniwa'' yet discovered. The burial facility is unclear, and details about the {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} are also unknown..<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404|qq=}}{{in lang|ja|qq=}}</ref> The tumulus is about a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Miwa Station|en|Miwa Station|qid=Q5362082|lt=Miwa Station|1=Miwa Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|qid=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Sakurai Line|en|Sakurai Line|qid=Q865801|lt=Sakurai Line|1=Sakurai Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/> <gallery> File:Chihara Ohaka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Top of the mound at the rear of the mound File:Chihara Ohaka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the mound at the rear from the front File:Chihara Ohaka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front from the rear </gallery> ==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|qid=Q17050860|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)}} ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} ==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Chihara Ohaka Kofun|qq=}} *[https://www.city.sakurai.lg.jp/sosiki/kyouikuiinkaijimukyoku/bunkazai/ka/1.html Sakurai City Tourist Association home page]{{in lang|ja|qq=}} *[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main00105.html Nara Prefecture History and Cultural database]{{in lang|ja|qq=}}
Chihara Ōbaka Kofun
{{Kofun navbar|qq=}}
